attributes.content:
## Overview

The Report card gives the workspace a structured home for data report with title, summary, charts, date range, and data sources.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Report turns raw activity into structured measurement. The card needs to stay queryable, filterable, and composable so that summaries, dashboards, and automations can build on top of it instead of recreating calculations downstream.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Metrics (KPI)]]** -- Metrics (KPI) can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Report.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Report can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Trigger]]** -- Changes to Report can fire automation, and automation can also create or update Report.
- **[[Project]]** -- Report can be grouped under a larger initiative so its work stays visible in context.
- **[[Survey]]** -- Survey can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Report.
- **[[Questionnaire]]** -- Questionnaire can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Report.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Report as the structured home for data report with title, summary, charts, date range, and data sources and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Report when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Report stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.

cardInfo.name: Report
cardInfo.summary: Data report with title, summary, charts, date range, and data sources.
